text: If overhead pressing is not tolerated, horizontal pressing or landmine-like angles are often easier to manage. The plan should avoid forcing end-range overhead work simply to keep variety.
title: Evidence Brief - Conservative Adaptations for Common Movement Restrictions

text: If deep knee flexion or high impact is limited, use box squats, partial range sit-to-stands, step-ups, or low-impact conditioning. Depth and tempo should remain symptom guided. High-impact intervals are not required for a general plan.
title: Evidence Brief - Conservative Adaptations for Common Movement Restrictions

text: When loaded lumbar flexion is a concern, choose neutral-spine hinge patterns, glute bridges, and trunk stability work. The goal is to preserve training exposure while reducing obvious mechanical conflict.
title: Evidence Brief - Conservative Adaptations for Common Movement Restrictions
